name: host-orchestrator
description: Host-side orchestration of the full AFK pipeline — implementation + merge — using Claude Code's native subagent + worktree primitives. Host-native only: no containers, no alternate substrate. Three slash commands (no auto-invocable skills, explicit invocation only): (1) `/parallel-implement-wave` dispatches 2-6 GitHub issues in parallel using `Agent(isolation: "worktree")` (no OAuth token gymnastics); (2) `/merge-orchestrate` runs intent-aware serial merge of 2-7 open PRs on host with `merge-resolver` Opus subagent enforcing 5 no-regression criteria; (3) `/afk-pipeline` thin per-turn playbook designed to be wrapped by Claude Code's native `/goal` command (v2.1.139+, Haiku verifier between turns) — checks state, picks next productive action (dispatch wave / merge wave / report blocker / declare done), updates PROGRESS.md + state.json, ends turn. Recommended entry point: `cc-afk <goal>` bash function (defined in README) that sets `CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW=180000`, `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TURNS=50`, `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_THINKING=1`, `API_TIMEOUT_MS=1200000`, then launches `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` with `/goal` wrapping `/afk-pipeline`. Custom subagents: `parallel-implementer` (Opus 4.8; TDD + vertical-slice + 7 anti-mock anti-patterns + bronze rule self-check) and `merge-resolver` (Opus; 5 no-regression criteria). Host owns all remote mutations. Audit log append-only. Smart-wave retry. Optional repo-local validation hook: `scripts/wave-validate.sh`. Renamed from `merge-orchestrator` v0.1.0 in v2.0.0; v2.1.0 adds `/afk-pipeline`; v2.3.0: /afk-pipeline gains a final review wave via /review-fleet --apply (engineering-workflow ≥ 2.6.0), fixes land as one `review/<slug>` PR; state.json gains a `review` key. v2.4.0: all three commands gain a blocking invocation gate — they run only when the user explicitly typed them (or inside an active cc-afk//goal AFK run). v2.5.0: `parallel-implementer` pinned to Opus 4.8 (`model: opus`; survives SendMessage resume, always available on the subscription). v3.0.0 (BREAKING): Docker/sandcastle substrate removed entirely — Leo decommissioned sandcastle-max; `/afk-pipeline` loses `--implement`/`--merge` flags (host is the only substrate); repo-local validation hook renamed `scripts/sandcastle-validate.sh` → `scripts/wave-validate.sh`; `cc-afk` and `cc-afk-host` are now equivalent.
